Y.description: The quantity of resource j that is accessible.

A.description: The quantity of electrical energy generated from trial i

I.description: Resource j quantity needed for experiment i.

assumptions.description: The electrical energy generated from each trial must be non-negative.

code.python: assert all(A[i] >= 0 for i in range(M))

assumptions.description: The quantity of each accessible resource must be non-negative.

code.python: assert all(Y[j] >= 0 for j in range(N))

assumptions.description: The resource quantity needed for each experiment must be non-negative.

code.python: assert all(I[j][i] >= 0 for j in range(N) for i in range(M))

assumptions.description: The number of resource types must be positive.
The number of experiment types must be positive.

j.description: The frequency at which each experiment is performed.

s.description: Slack variable for constraint: For each resource, the total amount required across all experiments does not exceed the available amount.

constraints.description: For each resource, the total amount required across all experiments plus slack equals the available amount.
constraints.formulation: \sum_{i=1}^{M} I_{k,i} \times j_i + s_k = Y_k \quad \forall k \in \{1, \ldots, N\}

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(quicksum(I[k][i] * j[i] for i in range(M)) + s[k] == Y[k] for k in range(N))

constraints.description: The frequency at which each experiment is performed must be non-negative.
The slack variable for each resource constraint must be non-negative.

objective.description: Increase the overall electrical output by conducting the experiments to their full potential.

code.gurobipy: model.setObjective(quicksum(j[i] * A[i] for i in range(M)), GRB.MAXIMIZE)

notes: Introduce slack variables to convert inequality constraints into equality constraints.
Source variation was missing the slack variables; we introduced them.
